what_it_does: Adaptive interview UI that collects a creative brief one question at a time, driven either by an AI interviewer (Vi/Claude) or a scripted engine, with voice/freeform input, file upload, a live understanding panel, in-place answer revision, and hooks into memory, context, and artifact systems.
observations: Implements a dual-mode interview: AI-driven via window.MS_INTAKE_AI.nextStep with graceful fallback to window.MS_INTAKE scripted engine.
Maintains a slide-like stage showing only the latest Vi lead and the current focused question, while the full exchange lives in a right-hand sequence rail.
answer() writes every captured answer to window.MS_MEMORY and window.MS_CONTEXT, and auto-creates an artifact when the selected value is a known MS_KINDS id.
Provides a feedback subsystem wiring via window.useFeedback, with redo() and currentQ() callbacks and rich questionTarget/messageTarget descriptors.
Records answered steps in a history array and supports reviewing/editing any previous answer in place without advancing the interview.
Exports QuestionBody on window so the unified StreamRunner can reuse the same question renderers.
Includes renderers for cards, multi-select, text, scale, upload, gather, dual constraints, and reference selection.
Renders Capabilities, HelpExpander, and Understanding panels that show confidence per intake area and a brief summary.
standouts: AI-driven interview with operator feedback that can regenerate the current question in place without advancing.
answer() synchronizes stated preferences into memory/context and immediately materializes selected generation types into artifacts.
Rich feedback target carries the full question card, private AI reasoning, understanding snapshot, and brief snapshot for discussion.
conventions: Uses unprefixed window globals (MS_INTAKE, MS_INTAKE_AI, MS_MEMORY, MS_CONTEXT, MS_KINDS, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_TOAST, MS_DATA, MS_RESOLVE, useFeedback, Composer, etc.) as cross-module integration points.
Aliases React hooks with an Iv suffix (useStateIv, useEffectIv, useRefIv, useCallbackIv).
Functional components with closure-defined helpers; imperative scroll and event handling mixed with React state.
Callback dependencies reference brief and transcript while also using a briefRef for latest values.
concerns: Many window.* dependencies are accessed without existence guards, so the component will throw if any subsystem is absent.
briefRef is updated both through React state and directly, creating risk of stale or duplicated state around AI brief patches.
answer() and fetchStep() both mutate the brief, so an AI step that returns a briefPatch plus a user answer may apply overlapping updates.
UploadAnswer creates object URLs via URL.createObjectURL but never revokes them, leaking memory when files are removed.
DualConstraints hardcodes the answer field as 'mustHave' and does not pass the avoid list to onAnswer, losing the 'never happen' selections.
applyVoiceToMove fallback only shows the freeform text in transcript but does not persist it as a brief note.
The mount useEffect unconditionally calls fetchStep when aiOn is true but has no cleanup to abort in-flight requests on unmount.
notes: This is the conversational brief-capture surface: it orchestrates question display, answer capture, memory/context mirroring, and artifact generation, with a right-rail view of the interview sequence and Vi's confidence.
questions: What is the exact contract/return shape expected from window.MS_INTAKE_AI.nextStep?
How is window.useFeedback implemented, and what does FeedbackLayer render?
What default values and shape does the brief prop have before any answers?
How does onTalk invoke voice capture and return transcribed text?
What is the lifecycle/cleanup contract for object URLs created from uploaded files?
How does MS_RESOLVE.instance resolve values for a given kind id?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file implements the described adaptive, one-question-at-a-time interview with AI/scripted paths, understanding panel, and generation gating.
apparent_themes: Human-AI co-design through conversational brief capture
Immediate materialization of choices into memory, context, and artifacts
intention_signals: Build a guided, AI-led intake experience that feels like an interview with a designer, continuously updates a shared brief and memory model, and turns early choices into real artifacts the user can inspect.
novelty: An AI interviewer that can be steered to re-ask the current question, auto-generates artifacts directly from card selections, and packages full question context plus private reasoning into feedback targets.
contribution: A concrete adaptive interview component plus a reusable set of question renderers, feedback wiring, and integration seams for memory, context, and artifact subsystems.
summary_for_embedding: intake.jsx is an adaptive, AI/scripted interview React component that captures a creative brief one question at a time, mirrors answers into memory and context, and can turn selected generation types into artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a conversational, feedback-aware brief-capture surface that keeps memory, context, and artifact generation synchronized as the user answers.

symbols.does: Builds a rich feedback/discussion target descriptor for the active question.
symbols.description: Collects the full question card content — Vi text, helper, options, recommendation, scale, optional flag, reasoning, understanding snapshot, brief snapshot, and context hint — into a structured target object for feedback or discussion.

symbols.does: Builds a feedback/discussion target descriptor for a Vi message.
symbols.description: Packages a Vi message's text, transcript index, current AI reasoning, brief snapshot, and context hint into a structured target object.

symbols.does: Returns a compact summary of the current brief for provenance.
symbols.description: Produces a short comma-joined string of known brief fields such as goal, buildType, and stakeholder, used to tag learning records with the moment they were captured.

symbols.does: Returns a readable, filtered snapshot of the brief so far.
symbols.description: Selects non-empty brief fields and formats array values as comma-separated strings, producing a clean object to hand to Vi or feedback systems when discussing a turn.

symbols.does: Closure helper that formats a single brief value for briefSnapshot.
symbols.description: Skips null, empty, or empty-array values and joins arrays into comma-separated strings, returning null for values that should be omitted from the snapshot.

symbols.does: Switch renderer that picks the input component for a question kind.
symbols.description: Dispatches to cards, multi, text, scale, upload, gather, dual, or refs renderers based on move.kind, and is exported on window so StreamRunner can reuse the same adaptive question UI.

symbols.does: Chip-based multi-select question renderer.
symbols.description: Renders selectable chips from move.options, enforces an optional max selection count, and calls onAnswer with the selected ids and joined labels.

symbols.does: Toggles an option id in the MultiSelect selection array.
symbols.description: Adds or removes the given id from the local selection state, dropping the oldest selection when the max limit would be exceeded.

symbols.does: Textarea question renderer with example chips.
symbols.description: Provides an auto-focused textarea, optional example chips that prefill the textarea, and submits on Cmd/Ctrl+Enter or button click.

symbols.does: Range-slider question renderer for scale questions.
symbols.description: Renders a numeric range input with min/max labels and clickable tick marks, then submits the chosen value as a density label.

symbols.does: Drag-and-drop file upload question renderer.
symbols.description: Provides a drop zone and hidden file input, tracks attached files with metadata and image previews via object URLs, and submits the file list.

symbols.does: Adds selected files to the UploadAnswer file list.
symbols.description: Converts a FileList into lightweight metadata objects including name, type, and an object URL for image previews, then appends them to local state.

symbols.does: Two-column chip selector for must/avoid constraints.
symbols.description: Renders separate chip groups for 'must be true' and 'never happen' options, tracking two local selection arrays and continuing with the must selection.

symbols.does: Generic toggle helper for DualConstraints selection arrays.
symbols.description: Returns a function that adds or removes an id from the provided array state, used for both must and avoid chip groups.

symbols.does: Grid selector for approved mockup/style-anchor references.
symbols.description: Reads approved and style-anchor mockups from window.MS_DATA, renders them as selectable thumbnails, and submits the selected reference ids.

symbols.does: Toggle helper for RefsAnswer selection.
symbols.description: Adds or removes a reference id from the local selected-refs array.

symbols.does: Source-intake question renderer.
symbols.description: Embeds a source-intake widget via WidgetHost, reads sources from the context store, and submits the sources the user wants to bring in.

symbols.does: Renders a capabilities panel when the user is unsure.
symbols.description: Displays a grid of intake capabilities from window.MS_INTAKE.CAPABILITIES to help the user see what kinds of things can be made.

symbols.does: Renders contextual help for the current question.
symbols.description: Shows tailored guidance for specific question ids like goal, goalNote, job, and task, falling back to generic reassurance for other questions.

symbols.does: Renders Vi's live understanding panel.
symbols.description: Shows an overall confidence meter, per-area confidence dots, and a brief summary with labeled lines for goal, intent, job-to-be-done, stakeholder, content, tone, and situation.

symbols.does: Small row component for the brief summary.
symbols.description: Renders a key/value pair in the understanding panel, showing a muted em-dash when the value is empty.

symbols.does: Resolves the brief's build type or legacy intent to a label.
symbols.description: Looks up brief.buildType in MS_INTAKE.BUILD_TYPES first, then falls back to brief.intent in MS_INTAKE.INTENTS, returning the matching label.

symbols.does: Merges an AI-interpreted brief patch into the existing brief.
symbols.description: Copies the existing brief, overwrites scalar fields, and merges arrays by deduplicating combined values, skipping null patch values.

symbols.does: Matches spoken/freeform text to the current question and answers it.
symbols.description: For text questions it submits the raw text; for cards/multi it matches option labels; otherwise it leaves the text as a transcript note without advancing.
